event_info: what drama first ball! Banged in short into the surface; this one hurried onto him as he shaped for the pull shot; the ball flew off the top-edge towards fine leg; Butler ran back and kept his eyes on the ball; but just couldn't hold onto it as it fell in front of him

ball_event: out Caught by Nishant Sindhu!! Siraj strikes and it's a massive blow for Punjab
event_info: excellent catch in the deep by the debutant Sindhu; short again outside off; Arya's eyes lit up as he slashes it in the air towards deep backward point; initially the ball looked like it was dying on Sindhu; but he dives well to his left and takes it just inches from the ground. Priyansh Arya c Nishant Sindhu b Mohammed Siraj 2(2)

ball_event: out Caught by Jos Buttler!! Punjab are in a disarray
event_info: top bowling from Siraj; excellent seam position; it was back of a length and swings in late; it almost cut him into half as he looked to defend; the ball takes the thinnest inside-edge back to the keeper; brilliant decision from the umpire; but Connolly challenges it; there was a spike on the UltraEdge and Punjab lose a review. Cooper Connolly c Jos Buttler b Mohammed Siraj 0(1)
back of a length delivery outside off; Iyer pushes it to the off side

he's off the mark with a cracking shot; back of a length and outside off; bit of width on offer; Iyer rides the bounce well and cuts it past the fielder to deep backward point; pure timing and it raced away
back of a length delivery outside off; Iyer knocks it towards point
he's off the mark as well; overpitched and in the slot; Singh doesn't miss out on those; gets to the pitch of the ball and creams it through covers; poor first ball from Rabada
streaky but he will take it; back of a length and outside off; this one bounced a bit; Singh slashes hard at it and the ball flew off the outside edge past the fielder to third man
short again outside off; Singh stands tall and cuts it square of the wicket; they run well for the second
back of a length and targeting the body; Singh hangs back and punches it to cover
1 run; drifting into the pads; Singh misses out on the clip shot and the ball rolls off the pads to backward square leg
terrific delivery to finish the over; back of a length and in the channel arond the off stump; this one moved away a bit; Iyer feels for it and misses; that was close to the outside edge
oh that kept a bit low and was dangerously close to the off stump; on a length and it comes back in; Singh was rooted to the crease; swings at it and misses
pitched up and outside off; Singh drives it to sweeper cover; it wasn't off the meat of the bat and they get a couple
angling into the pads; Singh works it away to deep square leg
test match length; around off stump and moving away late; Iyer gets right behind it and taps it to point
back of a length and angling in; Singh pushes it to mid-off and scrambles for a quick single
too full and sliding on the pads; Iyer clips it off his legs to backward square leg
play and a miss; good length delivery outside off and moving away; Iyer looks to defend and is beaten by the pace and the movement

excellent stop by Butler; angling down the leg side; Iyer misses out on it and he hits the pads with the bat; they sneak an extra run; Sudarshan at first slip has a shy at the striker's end and misses it; Shreyas was struggling at the non-striker's end had there been a direct hit
oh wild swing; good length outside off and moving away; Singh goes for an almighty swing and missed it completely; it was in his arc though
back of a length and targeting the body; Singh was hanging back in the crease; this one hurried onto him as he defends it to the off side
probing length around off stump from Rabada; Singh stands tall and pushes it to deep backward point

this off-side good bowling from Rabada compared to his first over; good length delivery around the off stump; Iyer looks to defend; but the ball goes towards the keeper; he wants the single; but sent back
length and outside off; Iyer knocks it to point for a quick single
full and swinging in; Iyer gets forward and defends it towards covers
poor delivery; full and drifting onto the pads; it's bread and butter for Iyer as he clips it effortlessly off his legs to deep mid-wicket
full again; but outside off; Iyer mistimes a drive towards mid off; it was in the air for a while; but fell short of the fielder
back of a length and angling in; Iyer stays back and punches it to mid on
back of a length and targeting the stumps; Iyer gets right behind the line and defends it
pitched up and in the slot; Iyer doesn't miss out on those; gets to the pitch of the ball and hits it through the line to long-off; he hit it in the air well wide of the fielder
he's flirting with danger; back of a length delivery outside off and this one climbed up a bit; Singh slashes hard at it away from the body and misses it

ball_event: out Caught by Manav Suthar!! He was struggling against Rabada tonight and this time he has hit it straight to the fielder
event_info: 152ks; full and outside off; Singh looks to go over cover; but was beaten by the pace as the bat turned in his hand and hits it straight to Manav; who takes the dolly. Prabhsimran Singh c Manav Suthar b Kagiso Rabada 15(14) [4s-2]
beaten first up; back of a length delivery in the channel around the off stump; this one moves away late; Wadhera was hanging back and is beaten all ends up
the effort ball; banged in short; hard into the pitch; Wadhera just manages to sway out of the line
back of a length outside off and swinging away; Wadhera just watches it go through; beat thing to do when Rabada is on top of his game

a wicket maiden folks; a rare feat in T20s; back of a length delivery outside off; Wadhera slays it on the up towards point; Washi dives to his left and saved a certain boundary; that was timed to perfection
angling into the pads; Iyer works it behind the wicket to backward square leg
this is good bowling; short and on middle; Wadhera gives him the charge; bit was beaten by the extra bounce

ball_event: out Caught by Jos Buttler!! GT are all over Punjab at the moment
event_info: brilliant catch from Butler; back of a length around the off stump and moving away; Wadhera was rooted to the crease as he pokes at it; the ball flew off the outside edge and Butler quickly dives to his left and takes a blinder. Nehal Wadhera c Jos Buttler b Jason Holder 0(6)
full and outside off; Shedge opens the face of the bat and guides it to deep backward point
short outside off; Iyer stands tall and pulls it down to long-on
short and outside off; Shedge stands tall and punches it to sweeper cover
back of a length and slanting across the right hander; Shedge gets right behind the line and defends it down on the track

streaky and Punjab need more of these; back of a length and outside off; Shedge goes for the pull; but was beaten by the extra bounce; the ball goes off the under edge past the diving Butler to third mna fence
sliding into the pads; Shedge flicks it to deep square leg
short again; on the pads; Iyer stands tall and works it to backward square leg
pitched up and in the slot; Shedge plays a full blooded drive straight to the fielder at mid off
back of a length and angled across; Shedge stays back and defends it solidly towards mid on
back of a length delivery outside off; Iyer cuts it straight to the fielder at point
back of a length and angling into the pads; Iyer pulls it to deep mid-wicket; just a single
back of a length and outside off; Shedge stands tall and punches it to sweeper cover

ball_event: out Bowled!! Holder has struck with a big wicket
event_info: he's over the moon; back of a length and angling in; around the off stump channel; Iyer was rooted to the crease and looked to guide it down to third man; may be he was done in by the extra bounce; drags it onto the stumps; Punjab are in a complete disarray. Shreyas Iyer b Jason Holder 19(21) [4s-3]

huge appeal was turned down; angling in from a good length; Stoinis was rooted to the crease as he looked to defend; huge inside edge have saved him; Holder was confident and urges Gill to go for the review; but a big inside edge and they lose the review
back of a length outside off; Stoinis gets right behind the line and defends it
to full outside off; Shedge drives it down to long-on

excellent effort by Gill at slip; tossed up delivery on middle; Stoinis gets down to sweep and misses it; the ball rolls off the pads towards leg slip and Gill quickly moves to his left and stops it; Stoinis wanted the single and was down the track; but comes back
tossed up delivery on middle; Stoinis drives it down to long-on
turning in; Shedge gets forward and works it to mid-wicket
fired in full on off; Stoinis attempts the reverse sweep and hits it towards point; doesn't connect properly
full and on middle; Stoinis eases it down to long-on; just 4 runs off it
130.9ks back of a length around off; Stoinis angles it with an open bat face to backward point
131.4ks short of length delivery; Stoinis rides the bounce and punches it to sweeper cover
fullish delivery; Shedge pushes it wide of mid-off and quickly crosses over
streaky! Punjab Kings will take them whichever way they come. This is not all that short for Stoinis to pull; but he still goes for it and the under-edge escapes past the stumps
that's more like it. 130.1ks short of length around off; Stoinis stays beside the line and opens the bat face; punches it square and beats the sweeper in the deep
128.8ks short and banged into the pitch; Stoinis rolls his wrists over the pull and places it wide of deep mid-wicket
89.5ks flighted delivery; too full and Stoinis squeezes the drive to long-off
94.4ks touch quicker on off; Shedge pushes it to long-off
overpitched and Stoinis drives it straight back; Rashid can't make the stop and Sindhu gets around; keeps it to a single
googly that's dragged short; Shedge rocks back and pulls on the bounce to deep mid-wicket
tossed up wrong 'un; Stoinis prods forward and blocks it back
93.1ks leg-break on middle; Stoinis eases it to the left of Rashid and he makes an acrobatic stop
back of a length delivery; Shedge moves away and cramps himself; swings across the line and misses

that's better from Shedge. First six of the game. He does not move around; stays balanced in the crease and picks the back of a length delivery off the hips; gets the bottom hand into play and sends it sailing over deep square leg
now charges down and is met by a shortened length; flays it over extra cover and it is not off the middle. Gill hunts it down from mid-off and keeps it to a couple
angled in from round the wicket; Shedge backs away and flicks it behind square on the leg-side
this is turning into a good over for Punjab Kings. Arshad goes short and down leg; easy pickings for Stoinis - swivels around and helps it along to the fine leg fence
now sprays a wayward bouncer outside off; Stoinis leaves and collects the wide
back of a length delivery; Stoinis pats it towards point and retains strike
tossed up and drifts in; Stoinis eases it past a diving Suthar and down to long-off
boundaries starting to flow.. This is flatter and quicker; but right in the slot for Shedge to free his hands; pumps it down the ground and clears long-on with ease
back to back sixes. This is higher and longer from Shedge. Full pitcher; around the half volley zone and Shedge launches it towards long-on. 91 meters maximum..

Suthar taken to the cleaners. 6; 6; 4! The pressure showing as he dishes out a juicy full toss outside off; Shedge just to had put it in the gap and he does so with ease; creams the drive in front of square and beats deep extra cover
more runs.. 6; 6; 4; 4! Shortens the length and offers width; Shedge reaches and cracks the cut in front of square. The fielder in the deep is at extra cover and he had no chance of stopping that

massive over - 27 runs. Did Suthar need to bowl? This is another friendly full toss and it is around leg-stump; free-hit for any batter and Shedge accepts it happily; heaves it high and long over deep mid-wicket
flatter delivery at the stumps; Stoinis forces it down the ground and Siraj keeps it to a single
googly on middle and leg; Shedge nudges it through mid-wicket and settles with a single
now Stoinis joins the party. This is flighted outside off; ample space on offer for Stoinis to extend those big arms; he muscles it down the ground and sends it bouncing in front of sightscreen
quicker and at the stumps; Stoinis knocks it down the pitch
short and outside off; Stoinis cuts it square and the sweeper tidies up

maiden IPL fifty for Shedge. Team in trouble; opposition right on top and he's soaked in the pressure to play a top knock. What's more he's reached the landmark in just 24 balls. He raises his bat and soaks in the applause of his team mates. Wrong 'un from Rashid; Shedge hangs back and works it into the vacant mid-wicket pocketT
even Rabada is not being spared. But this was a loosener - half volley down leg; Shedge picks it up nonchalantly and deposits it into the deep backward square stand
uhhh! On a length and outside off; Shedge swings and misses. That was there and Shedge will feel disappointed
146.2ks short and following a backing away Shedge; cramps him for room and the ball flies over the top-edge
slower ball bowled into the surface; Shedge is a bit early into the pull and gets a top-edge; the ball lands safely into the leg-side
146.4ks back of a length angled in; Stoinis checks his shot and lobs it past Shedge

ball_event: out Caught by Jos Buttler!! Soft dismissal! And Rabada has broken the partnership. A superb knock comes to an end. Rabada was brought back just for this and he delivers. Short of length delivery close to off
event_info: Shedge did not have the room to play the ramp or steer; gets an outside-edge and Buttler does the rest behind the stumps. Suryansh Shedge c Jos Buttler b Kagiso Rabada 57(29) [4s-3 6s-5]
good length ball on off; Stoinis stays behind the line and defends to the left of Siraj
129.1ks slower short ball; Stoinis attempts the upper cut but is deceived for pace; he misses and looks at the square leg umpire for a wide. Just one for the over comes the signal
142.4ks back of a length delivery; Stoinis pulls and gets it high on the bat; the ball lands short of Sai near long-on
fullish delivery at the stumps; Jansen pushes to mid-off and crosses over
nicely done! Short of length delivery; around off and Stoinis hops to get on top of the bounce; opens the bat face and steers it behind the square on off-side. No chance for third man

good review from Stoinis. He knew it was high. Short ball outside off; Stoinis is on his toes trying the upper cut but can't reach it. No signal from the umpire but Stoinis challenges the onfield call; gets it overturned..
full toss around off; Stoinis drives to long-off and retains strike
angled across from round the wicket; Stoinis guides it behind square and deep backward point tidies up
132.8ks good length ball; Jansen stays leg-side and mistimes the hack to long-on
just over mid-off. Full toss outside off; Stoinis has to reach for it and gets it off the toe-end. But the power he generates manages to evade the fielder inside the ring
short and angled outside off; Stoinis leaves and picks up the wide

ball_event: out Caught by Washington Sundar!! This is a massive moment. Stoinis knew and let out a grunt as soon as he hit it. Short of length angled across from round the wicket
event_info: rolled his fingers over the ball and Stoinis shuffles; looks to pull and gets it high on the bat; the mistimed shot carries to long-on and Washington makes no mistake. Punjab Kings needed Stoinis to bat through. Marcus Stoinis c Washington Sundar b Jason Holder 40(31) [4s-5 6s-1]

ball_event: out Bowled!! Cleaned up! Holder has a 4-fer. He's also on a hat-trick. What an addition he has been to this Gujarat Titans side! Player of the Match in the last game and now has four wickets. He goes a bit wide and angles it in
event_info: the ball deviates just enough off the pitch to beat Bartlett's inside-edge; goes onto hit the top of off-stump. That's a peach to get first up. Xavier Bartlett b Jason Holder 0(1)
132.4ks full and at the stumps; Vyshak defends from the crease and keeps it out the hat-trick ball. Holder finishes a superb spell. His figures read - 4-0-24-4
strays down leg; Jansen stays inside the line and misses the glance; wide called

dropped! The no-look shot from Jansen but it is all height; no distance and Suthar makes a mess. 140.9ks slot delivery around leg; Jansen keeps his head down and heaves it towards deep mid-wicket. Suthar gets around from deep square leg and goes with both hands but the ball pops out
short of length delivery; Jansen pulls it towards long-on
angled outside off; Vyshak tries the steer to third man and is beaten on the outside-edge
good length delivery; Vyshak squeezes it off the inside-edge to square leg
full toss outside off; Jansen stays leg-side and miscues the lofted drive over extra cover; clears Gill and picks up a couple
fullish delivery at the stumps; Jansen flicks to deep mid-wicket and keeps strike
flighted outside off; Jansen drives to long-off and denies the single
floated up outside off; Jansen uses his long reach and lofts it over extra cover; sweeper keeps it to a couple
there he goes. Once more Rashid keeps it outside off but Jansen manages to middle the loft; uses the long handle and lofts it down the ground; sends it sailing in front of the sightscreen
streaky! But they all count. Tossed up and very full; Jansen clears his front leg and swings hard; gets a thick inside-edge and the ball escapes past the leg-stump
tries to keep it away from his range and sprays it outside off; Jansen leaves and collects the wide

ball_event: out Caught by Shubman Gill!! Rashid wins the duel. But Jansen has played his part - 11-ball 20. This is short and outside off
event_info: once again Jansen has to reach for it and he slaps it towards extra cover. The ball was dying in front of Gill and he does well to bend down; scoops it up inches above the ground. Marco Jansen c Shubman Gill b Rashid Khan 20(11) [4s-1 6s-1]
googly on off; Arshdeep moves across and drags the pull to short fine
full and sliding on the pads; Sudharshan flicks it off his pads towards midwicket; a fumble by Vyshak and they come back for the second to mid-wicket

exquisite timing; pitched up and in the slot; Sudharsan presents full face of the bat; with high elbow position creams it on the up to deep extra cover; pleasing sight when a left hand plays that shot
good length and angling across; Sudharsan opens the face and guides it wide of the fielder to third man
back of a length and outside off; Sudharsan cuts it square of the wicket on the off side; the fielder runs in and cuts it off
full and pushed across; Sudharsan gets forward and slices it to deep backward point
leading edge; but he's off the mark; angling in from a length; Gill plays it across the line and the ball squirts off the edge to square third man
full and in the arc outside off; Gill reaches out and drives it towards covers; a half stop by the fielder and they don't go for the single
poor ball; short and angling across; Gill had ample time in the world to pull it to deep mid-wicket; you can't bowl short at such pace

ball_event: out Caught by Cooper Connolly!! The in-from Gill is gone
event_info: Singh has struck; full outside off and swinging in; Gill goes for it away from the body; beaten by the in-swing and ends up slicing it in the air towards point; where Cooper takes a well judged catch. Shubman Gill c Cooper Connolly b Arshdeep Singh 5(4) [4s-1]
he's back to bowling filth; full and sliding on the pads; Buttler clips it off his legs to backward square leg; Singh got away with it
back of a length outside off and angling across; Buttler watches it go through
full this time; Buttler square drives it straight to the fielder at point
targeting the stumps from a length; Sudharsan gets forward and defends it to the off side
angling in from outside off; Sudharsan defends it towards mid on
short and wide outside off stump; Sudharsan cuts it down to deep backward point

Jansen comes from round the wicket and angles into the right hander; Butler plays down the wrong line and is hit high on the pads; Jansen convinces Shreyas to go for the review; no bat involved and the ball tracker shows it was going over the top; Punjab lose a review
glorious from Buttler; pitched up and in the slot; Jansen goes full and Buttler takes full toll of it and dispatches it with utter disdain over wide long off
good length delivery outside off; Buttler defends it to the off side
good length delivery outside off; this one keeps a bit low; Sudharsan slashes hard and misses it
back of a length delivery outside off; Sudharsan pushes it to mid-on; Shreyas was bit late to the ball
back of a length and angling across; Buttler stands tall and punches it to cover
you can't bowl this when you have third man in the circle; short and climbing outside off; Sudharsan gets on top of the bounce nicely and upper cuts it over the fielder to third man
full this time and targeting the stumps; Sudharsan stays back and defends it watchfully
short outside off; Sudharsan stands tall and punches it off the back foot to sweeper cover; the fielder covers good ground and restricts them to a couple
full and tailing into the stumps; Buttler pushes it towards mid on
the Buttler special; length delivery outside off; Buttler moves across to the off side and just helps it on its way to fine leg; he made it look effortless; just scoops it using the pace
bit too full this time just outside off; Buttler once again attempts the paddle; but this time got it off the to-end of the bat
big inswinger from Bartlett; down the leg side; Buttler misses it and so does the diving keeper
exquisite timing; vintage Buttler on display; back of a length and outside off; Buttler waits for it on the back foot; gets on top of it nicely and just creams it on the up to sweeper cover
back of a length and this one jagged back in sharply; Buttler just manages to get some inside edge onto the pads and the ball rolls away towards point

did he get some bat on it or it brushed the pad on its way? good length delivery swinging down the leg side; Sudharsan misses out on the flick; no wide signalled and Bartlett goes up; GT doesn't review for the wide; strange!
back of a length and on off; Buttler moves across and once again attempts the lap shot; but missed it completely
50 up for GT; back of a length and angling in; Buttler works it to square leg
banged in short on the stumps; it was moving down the leg side; Sudharsan goes for the pull and misses it; a stifled appeal for the catch; but the umpire signals a wide
back of a length delivery just outside off; it moves away late; Sudharsan feels for it and is beaten all ends up
back of a length delivery outside off; Sudharsan defends it to the off side

this time he's gotton hold of the short ball; banged in short on middle; Sudharsan gets into the position nicely and pulls it over fine leg; that went 30 rows back in the stands; he used Jansen's pace to full effect
back of a length outside off; Sudharsan taps it towards deep backward point; they end the powerplay on a good note
back of a length and sliding on the pads; Sudharsan works it off his hips to deep square leg
back of a length and outside off; Buttler opens the face and guides it down to third man
short and on the stumps; Sudharsan mistimes a pull to deep mid-wicket
this one jags back in sharply; Buttler got into a tangle as he was looking to defend; the ball goes off the inside edge towards the off side and they scamper for a quick single
back of a length and angling across; Sudharsan cuts it to sweeper cover

1 run; This one is full and tailing into the pads; Vyshak was confident immediately and forces Shreyas to go for the review; no bat involved and the ball tracking shows it was missing the leg stump; Punjab lose both the reviews
back of a length delivery outside off; Buttler defends it to the off side
full and outside off; Buttler walks down the track and knocks it down to long-on
short outside off; Sudharsan slays it on the up to point; good stop from Arya as he saved a certain boundary; the partnership between Sai Sudharsan and Jos Buttler is now 50
full on off and middle; Buttler drives it straight down the ground to long-on
full and straying on the pads; Sudharsan clips it to backward square leg; initial call was for two; but they settle for the single
Buttler makes room towards the legside; Stoinis follows him with a back of a length delivery; Buttler adjusts it in the end and mistimes a pull to deep mid-wicket

ball_event: out Caught by Shreyas Iyer!! Big wicket and Vyshak has struck after burning a review against him in his last over
event_info: good length delivery outside off; it was the slower one; Buttler had back away to the leg side to play it inside out over cover; but was foxed by the lack of pace and ballons it in the air to Shreyas; who pumps his fist in excitement. Jos Buttler c Shreyas Iyer b Vijaykumar Vyshak 26(22) [4s-1 6s-2]
fuller length delivery outside off; Sindhu drives it to sweeper cover
pitched up and in the slot; Sudharsan plays the full blooded drive; but Stoinis at covers; dives to his left and half stops it; he certainly saved a boundary
back of a length delivery outside off; Sindhu defends it to the off side
short and outside off from round the wicket; Sindhu stands tall and plays it with soft hands to point
Vyshak has got away with it; back of a length and on the pads; Sudharsan flicks it off his legs straight to the fielder at short fine
back of a length and angling into the pads; Sudharsan works it towards short midwicket
back of a length and outside off; Sudharsan dabs it down to third man
short and outside off; Sindhu stands tall and punches it in the gap to sweeper cover; the deep fielder covers good ground and cuts it off
back of a length and targeting the stumps; cramped for any room; Sindhu gets right behind the line and defends it

strange shot; banged in short on the stumps; Sindhu gets into an awkward position as he was beaten for pace; hangs his bat around and the ball takes the edge and goes towards fine leg; it was in the air for a moment; but landed safely
back of a length and outside off; Sudharsan taps it to point
full and angled across; Sudharsan drives square and picks out sweeper
fullish delivery outside off; Sindhu shuffles and drags the hack towards deep mid-wicket; just a couple

first boundary in 28 balls. Sudharsan had a word with Sindhu just before this ball. Not sure what was told but it has worked. Short of length delivery; nicely sits up and Sindhu nails the pull over backward square leg. He got into a good position and nailed the shot perfectly
back of a length around off; Sindhu knocks it in front of mid-off and quickly crosses over
good length ball outside off; Sudharsan reaches for the square drive and is beaten on the outside-edge. Checks with Sindhu about the line and decides against the review

big over - 14 runs. Was a third over needed for Vyshak? Yes; he had bowled well in the first two but he gives two freebies in this over. Short and outside off; Sudharsan does not bother to keep the cut down - goes up and wide of backward point; giving third man no chance of stopping
back of a length close to off; Sindhu jams it onto the pad and the ball rolls into the off-side

short and down leg; Sudharsan backs away and pulls backward of square. Arshdeep and Shedge combine to keep it to a single. That's poor running. Arshdeep had to run across; tumble and stop before Shedge threw the ball back; enough time for a second

ball_event: out Caught by Vijaykumar Vyshak!! Excellent catch by Vyshak. Stoinis breaks the partnership. Just when Gujarat Titans were starting to get some momentum into the innings
event_info: Stoinis strikes. Short and outside off; it was bouncing and Sindhu went for the upper cut; gets more height than distance. Vyshak runs to his left from third man and takes a super catch. Nishant Sindhu c Vijaykumar Vyshak b Marcus Stoinis 15(11) [6s-1]
fullish delivery on leg; Washington gently turns it to mid-wicket
Stoinis goes short and into the pitch; but Washington is taller than Sindhu; he jumps up and slashes the cut over backward point. He played that late and ensured it was wide of Vyshak at third man
back of a length delivery; not quite short and Washington mistimes the pull towards deep mid-wicket
back of a length around off; Washington turns it wristily to long-on
132.1ks too straight - on middle and leg; Sudharsan tucks it in front of square
on a length and outside off; Washington guides it to backward point
134.2ks short of length close to off; Washington rides the bounce and knocks it to cover
three dots in a row. Washington once again picks out the cover fielder
136.2ks full and angling into the pads; Washington flicks in front of square and picks up a brace
flatter and leg-lined; Sudharsan nudges it with soft hands and races back for the second

googly around leg; Sudharsan sits down and gets an under-edge on the attempted sweep; the ball goes fine and Arshdeep's effort is not good enough - he dives in installments and allows it to escape past him near short fine
wrong 'un that's on the shorter side; Sudharsan nudges it through mid-wicket
sticks to the leg-stump line; Washington tucks it square on the leg-side

fifty for Sai Sudharsan. He is anchoring this chase nicely and needs to stay till the end. He acknowledges the applause by pointing to his bat and giving the thumbs up. Chahal is trying to not give room for the left-handers to open their arms and in the process drifting down leg; Sudharsan kneels down and sweeps it behind square; giving the fielder in the deep no chance
flatter and at the stumps; Sudharsan flicks it to deep mid-wicket and settles with a single
131.3ks short and outside off; Sudharsan cracks it square and the sweeper mops up

that's more like it. The duo getting a move on now. Fullish delivery angled across; Washington had the width to open his arms and though the lofted drive is mistimed; he places it wide of mid-off and picks up the boundary
good length ball around off; Washington knocks it to mid-off and quickly sets off. Luckily Iyer does not gather the ball and allows Washington to get in comfortably
short of length close to off; Sudharsan plays it late and chops it hard into the ground; the ball bounces wide of backward point and Shedge has some ground to cover from sweeper; comfortable brace

ball_event: out Caught by Xavier Bartlett!! Vyshak strikes back! He had gone for runs in this over but has got the big wicket of Sudharsan. Once again just when Gujarat Titans had started to pull away
event_info: Punjab Kings strike back. Short ball that gets big on Sudharsan as he takes it on; the pull is miscued towards deep mid-wicket and Bartlett takes a well-judged catch. He didn't have to move much but ensured he's well-balanced and took it above his head just inside the rope. Sai Sudharsan c Xavier Bartlett b Vijaykumar Vyshak 57(41) [4s-5 6s-1]
back of a length in the channel; Tewatia looks to defend and is beaten on the outside-edge. Vyshak finishes a fine spell. His figures read - 4-0-31-2
good length ball around off; Washington pushes it to mid-off and quickly crosses over
134.8ks back of a length delivery; Tewatia swivels and pulls to long leg
131.8ks fullish delivery around off; Washington pushes it off the inner half towards mid-wicket
136.1ks back of a length delivery; Tewatia dabs it in front of backward point and wants a single; sent back by Washington
1 run; length delivery around leg; Tewatia misses the heave and the ball deflects off the pad
slower short ball angled across; Washington hops and attempts to swat it into the leg-side; he's beaten by the lack of pace
130.9ks too straight - on middle and leg; Tewatia works it to deep mid-wicket

not where he intended. But Gujarat Titans will take it. Gill even has a smile on his face. Fullish delivery close to off; Washington clears his front leg and looks to go leg-side; gets a thick outside-edge and the ball races away fine

back to back fours. Washington shuffles across and bends down to play the scoop. Once again does not middle it but gets enough to clear Vyshak running back from short fine. He gives chase and dives head first to pull the ball back in but it rolls back; and his attempt to keep it in play goes in vain
fullish delivery around off; Washington clips it in the gap between long-on and deep mid-wicket; calls for two and Tewatia had to put in the dive to make his ground
134.1ks leg-lined delivery; Washington flicks to deep square leg

ball_event: out Caught by Xavier Bartlett!! This game ain't over. Gujarat Titans are five down and Tewatia sits there in disbelief. He struck that well but also picked out the fielder perfectly. Good length ball outside off
event_info: Tewatia sits down and absolutely nails the sweep; smashed it flat and straight to deep sqaure leg. Bartlett didn't move an inch to take that - came right at him and pouched it safely. Rahul Tewatia c Xavier Bartlett b Marco Jansen 2(6)

shot! Whenever there's a bit of pressure Washington is getting that boundary to release it. Short of length delivery; he gets on top of the bounce and pulls it in front of square; splitting deep mid-wicket and long-on perfectly
126.4ks short and at the body; Washington hops inside the line and gets on top of the bounce to pull to deep square leg
good length ball outside off; Holder meets it on top of the bounce and caresses it past a diving cover fielder. Long chase for Shedge in the deep and the pair pick up two more
134.8ks on a length and around off; Holder pushes it on the up and picks out mid-off
fullish delivery and Holder drives it towards cover; can't beat Connolly

three dots to end the over. That's excellent from Bartlett. He went for 7 off the first three balls and 7 it will be from the over. Another full delivery and Holder drives down the ground. Bartlett bends down and stops in his followthrough
132.2ks good length delivery; Washington drives to long-off

angled in from back of a length; Holder turns it with soft hands to mid-wicket and calsl for two. He had to hurry as Connolly went to the striker's end. His long legs save him as he gets in before Prabhsimran lights up the stumps
131.2ks on a length and close to off; Holder pats it to backward point and crosses over
not all that short but Washington goes for the pull; mistimes it to long-on

ball_event: out Caught by Cooper Connolly!! What a catch! Under pressure Connolly has pulled off a brilliant catch. Holder departs and this game is back in the balance. Good length ball
event_info: angled across and Holder clears his front leg; heaves it towards cow corner. Connolly runs around from deep mid-wicket; keeps his eyes on the ball and takes it with both hands; he slides and goes to ground to ensure he has a better grip of the ball. Iyer was right next to him as he ran across from long-on and as soon as the catch was completed; he embraced him in a hug. Jason Holder c Cooper Connolly b Arshdeep Singh 5(7)
138ks good length ball; Arshad heaves it to long-on and retains strike
slower bouncer; but too short and well over Arshad; he watches it sail over him and umpire signals wide for height

that's unlucky for Stoinis. He nails the yorker around leg-stump; Arshad clears his front leg and squeezes it off the inside-edge backward of square. The ball had enough to trickle into the rope. 6 from 5

just short of Shedge. Full delivery outside leg; Arshad flicks it with a strong bottom hand backward of square. The ball was in the air and Shedge sprinted to his left; dived and the ball landed just in front of him. 4 from 4
short and outside off; Arshad throws the kitchen sink on the slash and misses. That's a dot. 4 off 3

ufff! What a ball! Stoinis could not have nailed the yorker any better. Arshad jams it onto his right boot and the ball rolls down the pitch. Washington calls him across and Stoinis picks up the ball; takes aim and does not hit the target at the non-striker's end. Direct hit might have had Arshad. 3 off 2

Gujarat Titans win by 4 wickets. They hold their nerve and seal the victory with a ball to spare. Stoinis goes for the yorker and does not quite nail it this time. It is a juicy full toss; Washington premeditated the scoop and gets it high on the bat; but enough for the ball to sail over the rope at deep backward square
